payload.description: CHAR_B: "Okay. Every single person in here is looking at us."

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Okay. Every single person in here is looking at us.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: No. They're looking at the concept of us. Different thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That's not better.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I didn't say it was better. I said it was different.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Why are we even doing this together. Give me one real reason.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: We have complementary skill sets.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: What's my skill set.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You go places. I make the places weird. Unstoppable combo.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Good. You're both here. I told them you'd be here.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Told who.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Doesn't matter. What matters is the door is open.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: What door. I only see a wall.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Exactly. So we work fast.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: This was supposed to be ready.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: It's not ready.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I can see it's not ready. Someone fix it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I could fix it. Or I could make it more interestingly broken.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Do NOT.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You ever feel like every room you walk into is already watching?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: ...Yeah.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Does it bother you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Only when they decide they understand me.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Okay. New plan. Smaller. Weirder. Both of you just do exactly what you do.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That's not a plan.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: It's always worked before.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I respect the chaos-to-strategy ratio here.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: So we're actually doing this.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I've been doing it since narreme one.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That's not a thing you can say.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You're right. And yet.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: They're still watching.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Let them.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Honestly? Being watched is fine. Being understood is the overstep.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I'm putting that on a poster.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Please do not.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Too late. It's already happening.

state.conditioning_notes: `<CHAR_A>`'s love of breaking the pattern and fear of being taken too seriously shape every exchange — the surreal pivots ("the concept of us"), the retreat when `<CHAR_B>` gets genuinely close in narreme_005, the refusal to let the moment become a clean lesson. `<CHAR_B>`'s love of the unknown and fear of being stuck drives the friction with the public arena — they want to move, not perform; the reluctant_partners dynamic forces them to hold position, which costs them. `<CHAR_C>`'s love of making things happen and fear of being all talk are baked into the "door is open / we work fast" energy and the pivot in narreme_006 where a broken plan becomes a new plan before anyone can call the first one a failure. The Arena location's watched, performative pressure stays in the background of every beat — characters are alert, self-aware, hyper-conscious of the audience, without ever describing the audience directly. The falling arc traces: chaos under maximum scrutiny → a quiet crack → reluctant alignment → the crowd becomes irrelevant.
